data_accessibility.0: Data is siloed, manual export only, or inaccessible programmatically.
data_accessibility.1: Data is accessible via API or DB but fragmented across systems.
data_accessibility.2: Data is centralized, queryable, and actively integrated.

data_quality_history.0: Data is untrusted, unlabelled, or lacks historical depth.
data_quality_history.1: Data is mostly clean but lacks deep history or comprehensive labels.
data_quality_history.2: Data is clean, labelled, governed, and has strong historical depth.

systems_integration.0: Core systems are disconnected; manual data entry prevails.
systems_integration.1: Some systems integrated point-to-point; partial automation.
systems_integration.2: Systems fully integrated via API/ESB; strong platform coherence.

use_case_specificity.0: Vague aspiration ('we want AI'); no measurable problem defined.
use_case_specificity.1: Specific problems identified but ROI/metrics not yet validated.
use_case_specificity.2: Highly specific use cases with clear success metrics and ROI.

implementation_capability.0: No internal tech capability; waterfall delivery model.
implementation_capability.1: Basic IT capability; transitioning to agile delivery.
implementation_capability.2: Strong technical capability; mature agile and product ownership.

adoption_conditions.0: Workforce resistant or lacking basic digital literacy.
adoption_conditions.1: Workforce capable but lacks structured change management.
adoption_conditions.2: High digital literacy; mature change management in place.

leadership_sponsorship.0: No executive mandate; viewed as an IT experiment.
leadership_sponsorship.1: Executive support exists but budget/accountability unclear.
leadership_sponsorship.2: Strong board mandate, clear budget, named executive sponsor.
